content:
3 min read
Just now

--

Reality is meant to be discovered. What is real? What is truth? A machine cannot discern these things…only the human mind. We are structured to learn and discern between good and bad. Correct and incorrect. Truth and lies. As human beings, we experience a lifetime of learning. But recalling names and places becomes challenging as we age.
Ideas come like a flash of light while mindful thought ferrets out possibilities and instinctively develops logical results. What once seemed impossible becomes a working reality. We work at experimenting, while quietly dismantling mistakes. That is our way.
Solutions emerge from discoveries and inventions inspired by others, already in use. The structure of the wheel, mechanics of the automobile, mined ores of railroad tracks, aerodynamics of the airplane, mathematics, etc.
Ideas can be far-fetched.
Lots of criticism is made of Elon Musk. Yet, Elon Musk is a visionary with the imagination to foresee future trends or developments. His ideas stretch into the reality of people living and breathing on Mars. Yet, mankind did not “create” planets. God did. Although Musk’s dream of landing a spaceship on Mars bears little fruit because he cannot live long enough to rearrange the elements of that planet, he still floats the idea. Musk’s success with Starlink benefits mankind. Also, he is one of the co-founders of Neuralink, the developer of high-bandwidth brain-computer interfaces that connect human brains with computers. Although some of these ideas seem a bit far-fetched, the roots might produce favorable results in other areas.
As the inventions of mankind and mathematical codes enable devices to assist our lifestyles, we find ourselves in the midst of a montage of developers attempting to push the limits of accurate coding. As models appear on the Internet, the question now is: are you willing to adapt its inaccuracies and/or solutions into your lifestyle?
Computer Vision Syndrome
It is said that the Internet exposes us to real risks and that excessive participation in social media causes anxiety and depression. Walking around with a phone in the face is intoxicating, yet debilitating. Because we spend too much time on the Internet, physical health issues like dry eyes, headaches, blurry vision, neck pain, muscle pain, and disrupted sleep are causing setbacks. Thus, we are advised to limit screen activity time to about two hours per day.
Use it or Lose it
Thinking for oneself invigorates the brain. This is a good reason to stay active, mentally and physically.
School Learning
Did you know that reading, writing, and multiplication tables are generally recalled throughout our lifetime? When in grammar school participating in spelling bees, I learned an invaluable technique for “remembering.” I am in my golden years now, and use this same method to help remember names, events, dates, etc.

• Pronounce the word aloud.

• Write it down.

• Spell it.
The theory is that if you are staring at a computer all day, instead of thinking and resolving, you are losing your memory, pal! Yet society has its little “fixes,” like crossword and picture puzzles, verbal conversations, reading, writing, adding, multiplying, etc. Issues resolved by thinking and analyzing are a good thing. Thus, the struggles in one’s life could elevate the human mind into unenvisioned success!
Cognivue Health and Memory Loss
Our brains control everything we think, feel, remember, and do. Several years ago, when I purchased a hearing aid, the audiologist screened my brain health with a computerized system called Cognivue. This test is standard now.
